title: L4: Data Preparation & Formatting
description: Prepare spreadsheet data for BTEC DIT Component 2 by sorting safely, using clear formatting, applying validation choices and highlighting important records.

steps.title: Do Now: Previous Skills
steps.content:
Do now

These questions check the formula and function skills you need before preparing a dataset.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l4-dn',[{q:'A spreadsheet needs to add all Income values from D2 to D10. Which formula is best?',options:['=SUM(D2:D10)','=D2+D10','=COUNT(D2:D10)','=TOTAL(D2:D10)'],answer:0,right:'Correct. SUM is used to total a range.'},{q:'Which function would count how many Region cells say Online?',options:['COUNTIF','COUNT','SUMIF','IF'],answer:0,right:'Correct. COUNTIF counts cells that match a condition.'},{q:'Which formula adds Income values in C2:C20 only where the Sales Method in B2:B20 is Shop?',options:['=SUMIF(B2:B20,"Shop",C2:C20)','=COUNTIF(B2:B20,"Shop")','=SUM(C2:C20,"Shop")','=IF(B2:B20="Shop",C2:C20)'],answer:0,right:'Correct. SUMIF checks one range and adds the matching values from another.'},{q:'Why might B$7 be used in a copied percentage formula?',options:['To keep row 7 fixed when the formula is copied down','To format the cell as money','To make the formula hidden','To count row 7 only'],answer:0,right:'Correct. The dollar sign locks the row reference.'},{q:'In =IF(C2>350,"Top Seller","Standard"), what appears if C2 is 200?',options:['Standard','Top Seller','FALSE','200'],answer:0,right:'Correct. 200 is not above 350, so the false result is shown.'}]);

steps.title: Why Prepare Data?
steps.content:
Why prepare data?

BTEC Component 2 tasks often ask you to complete analysis and dashboard worksheets. Before the dashboard is useful, the data must be easy to read, consistent and safe to use.

Preparation skillWhy it matters
Sort the full rowsKeeps each record together while changing the order.
Currency and percentage formattingMakes values clear and professional.
Validation listsReduces typing mistakes by limiting choices.
Conditional formattingMakes important records stand out.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l4-purpose',[{q:'A student sorts only the Cost column and not the rest of the row. What is the problem?',options:['The costs no longer match the correct items','The file becomes too large','All formulas are deleted','The worksheet cannot be saved'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Sorting one column by itself breaks data integrity.'},{q:'Why format money to currency with 2 decimal places?',options:['So values such as 12.5 display clearly as money','So all formulas disappear','So the sheet sorts automatically','So text becomes uppercase'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Currency formatting improves clarity without changing the value.'},{q:'Why use a drop-down list for a field such as Sales Method?',options:['It prevents inconsistent typing such as Shop, shop and Shops','It calculates the total automatically','It creates a chart','It locks the whole worksheet'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Validation makes entries consistent.'}]);

steps.title: Sort Safely
steps.content:
Sort safely

When an exam asks you to sort data, you must sort the whole row, not just one column. This keeps the item, type, cost and sales method together.

Sort instructions can also have two levels. For example: sort by Item type A to Z, and within each type sort by Cost low to high.

RecordItem typeCost
ABinder20.50
BDiary10.00
CBinder8.50
DDiary6.00

If you sort by Item type, then Cost, the correct order is Binder 8.50, Binder 20.50, Diary 6.00, Diary 10.00.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l4-sort',[{q:'Which record should appear first after sorting by Item type A-Z, then Cost low-high?',options:['C - Binder - 8.50','A - Binder - 20.50','D - Diary - 6.00','B - Diary - 10.00'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Binder comes before Diary, and 8.50 is the lowest Binder cost.'},{q:'Which record should appear last?',options:['B - Diary - 10.00','D - Diary - 6.00','A - Binder - 20.50','C - Binder - 8.50'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Diaries come after Binders, and 10.00 is the higher Diary cost.'},{q:'What must be selected before sorting a dataset?',options:['The full table/range of records','Only the column being sorted','Only the title row','Only blank cells'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Select the full data range so rows stay intact.'}]);

steps.title: Formatting Values
steps.content:
Formatting values

Formatting changes how a value looks, not what it is. In BTEC spreadsheets, common formatting requests include currency with 2 decimal places and percentages.

Raw valueGood formatWhy
1732.129999£1,732.13Money should be currency with 2dp.
0.4242%Percentages should look like percentages.
12.5£12.50The zero matters for money.

Now practise calculating values that should later be formatted clearly. Use formulas, not typed answers.
steps.js: (function(){function lcss(u){if(!document.querySelector('link[href="'+u+'"]')){var l=document.createElement('link');l.rel='stylesheet';l.href=u;document.head.appendChild(l);}}function ljs(u,cb){var ex=document.querySelector('script[data-jhncc-src="'+u+'"]');if(ex){if(ex.getAttribute('data-loaded')==='1'){cb();}else{ex.addEventListener('load',function(){cb();});}return;}var s=document.createElement('script');s.setAttribute('data-jhncc-src',u);s.src=u;s.onload=function(){s.setAttribute('data-loaded','1');cb();};s.onerror=function(){alert('The spreadsheet tool could not load. Please refresh the page.');};document.head.appendChild(s);}lcss('assets/css/jsuites.css');lcss('assets/css/jspreadsheet.css');ljs('assets/js/jsuites.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet-formula-bar.js?v=22',function(){var data=[["Label","Value"],["Total income",1732.13],["Shop income",888.73],["% shop sales",""]];var sheet=jspreadsheet(document.getElementById('l4-format-sheet'),{data:data,columns:[{width:150},{width:120}],minDimensions:[2,4],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(document.getElementById('l4-format-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddFormatToolbar(document.getElementById('l4-format-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddSheetTabs(document.getElementById('l4-format-sheet'),sheet);document.getElementById('l4-format-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l4-format-fb');var raw=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(1,3,false)||'');var val=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(1,3,true));if(raw.charAt(0)!=='='){fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='Use a formula in B4. Divide Shop income by Total income, then format B4 as Percentage.';return;}if(raw.toUpperCase().indexOf('B3')===-1||raw.toUpperCase().indexOf('B2')===-1){fb.className='ex-feedback hint';fb.textContent='Your formula should use the cells B3 and B2, not typed numbers.';return;}var fmt=(sheet._jhnccNumberFormats&&sheet._jhnccNumberFormats.B4&&sheet._jhnccNumberFormats.B4.type)||'general';if(Math.abs(val-0.5131)>0.003||fmt!=='percent'){fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Not quite. Shop income divided by total income is about 0.5131, displayed as 51.31% when formatted as Percentage.';return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Correct. The formula calculates a proportion and B4 is formatted as a percentage.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: Validation & Highlighting
steps.content:
Validation and highlighting

Some BTEC tasks ask you to make a data field more reliable or to make a value stand out. Two common tools are data validation and conditional formatting.

ToolUse it when...Example
Data validation listThe user should choose from allowed options.Sales Method: Shop or Online
Conditional formattingCells should stand out when they match a rule.Highlight all rows where Sales Method is Shop
steps.js: createMCQSet('l4-valid',[{q:'A field must only allow Shop or Online. Which tool is most suitable?',options:['Data validation list','SUMIF','Sort descending','Currency formatting'],answer:0,right:'Correct. A validation list limits entries to allowed choices.'},{q:'A task says to make Shop sales stand out. Which tool is most suitable?',options:['Conditional formatting','COUNT','AVERAGE','Freeze panes'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Conditional formatting highlights cells that meet a rule.'},{q:'Which condition would highlight income values of 500 or more?',options:['Greater than or equal to 500','Contains 500 letters','Less than 500','Equal to blank'],answer:0,right:'Correct. The rule should match values at least 500.'},{q:'Why should validation choices be consistent with the existing data?',options:['So formulas such as COUNTIF and SUMIF match the text correctly','So the file opens faster','So all charts become pie charts','So rows are deleted automatically'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Inconsistent text breaks condition-based analysis.'}]);

steps.title: Full Practice
steps.content:
Full practice: prepare analysis values

Complete the shaded analysis cells. This mirrors the kind of preparation needed before values are formatted and used on a dashboard.

CellTask
E7Total income for all sales
E8Total income for Shop sales only
E9Percentage of income from Shop sales
E10Number of Online sales
steps.js: (function(){function lcss(u){if(!document.querySelector('link[href="'+u+'"]')){var l=document.createElement('link');l.rel='stylesheet';l.href=u;document.head.appendChild(l);}}function ljs(u,cb){var ex=document.querySelector('script[data-jhncc-src="'+u+'"]');if(ex){if(ex.getAttribute('data-loaded')==='1'){cb();}else{ex.addEventListener('load',function(){cb();});}return;}var s=document.createElement('script');s.setAttribute('data-jhncc-src',u);s.src=u;s.onload=function(){s.setAttribute('data-loaded','1');cb();};s.onerror=function(){alert('The spreadsheet tool could not load. Please refresh the page.');};document.head.appendChild(s);}lcss('assets/css/jsuites.css');lcss('assets/css/jspreadsheet.css');ljs('assets/js/jsuites.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet.js',function(){ljs('assets/js/jspreadsheet-formula-bar.js?v=22',function(){var data=[["Item","Type","Method","Income","Analysis"],["A5 Diary","Diary","Shop",300,""],["Beach Binder","Binder","Online",820,""],["Budget Pen","Pen","Shop",120,""],["Desk Pad","Pad","Online",450,""],["Executive Pen","Pen","Shop",240,""],["","","","Total income",""] ,["","","","Shop income",""] ,["","","","% Shop income",""] ,["","","","Online count",""]];var sheet=jspreadsheet(document.getElementById('l4-full-sheet'),{data:data,columns:[{width:130},{width:90},{width:90},{width:110},{width:120}],minDimensions:[5,10],tableOverflow:true,tableWidth:'100%',toolbar:false,about:false});JHNCCAddFormulaBar(document.getElementById('l4-full-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddFormatToolbar(document.getElementById('l4-full-sheet'),sheet);JHNCCAddSheetTabs(document.getElementById('l4-full-sheet'),sheet);document.getElementById('l4-full-check').onclick=function(){var fb=document.getElementById('l4-full-fb');var checks=[{r:6,fn:'SUM',exp:1930,need:['D2:D6']},{r:7,fn:'SUMIF',exp:660,need:['C2:C6','Shop','D2:D6']},{r:8,fn:null,exp:0.342,need:['E8','E7'],fmt:'percent'},{r:9,fn:'COUNTIF',exp:2,need:['C2:C6','Online']}];var errors=[];checks.forEach(function(c){var raw=String(sheet.getValueFromCoords(4,c.r,false)||'');var norm=raw.toUpperCase().replace(/\s+/g,'');var val=parseFloat(sheet.getValueFromCoords(4,c.r,true));if(raw.charAt(0)!=='=')errors.push('E'+(c.r+1)+' needs a formula');else if(c.fn&&norm.indexOf(c.fn+'(')===-1)errors.push('E'+(c.r+1)+' should use '+c.fn);else if(c.need.some(function(x){return norm.indexOf(String(x).toUpperCase().replace(/\s+/g,''))===-1;}))errors.push('E'+(c.r+1)+' is missing an expected range or criteria');else if(Math.abs(val-c.exp)>(c.fmt?0.006:0.6))errors.push('E'+(c.r+1)+' gives '+val+' but should be about '+c.exp);else if(c.fmt&&(!sheet._jhnccNumberFormats||!sheet._jhnccNumberFormats['E'+(c.r+1)]||sheet._jhnccNumberFormats['E'+(c.r+1)].type!==c.fmt))errors.push('E'+(c.r+1)+' should be formatted as Percentage');});if(errors.length){fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Issues: '+errors.join(' | ');return;}fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent='Correct. These analysis values are ready to format and present clearly.';__markStepComplete();};});});});})();

steps.title: Check What You Have Learned
steps.content:
Check what you have learned

These questions check the preparation skills used in BTEC spreadsheet tasks.
steps.js: createMCQSet('l4-exam',[{q:'What does data integrity mean when sorting?',options:['Each row still keeps the correct values together','The sheet has lots of colours','The data is copied into Word','The chart has a title'],answer:0,right:'Correct. Sorting must not separate values from their original record.'},{q:'Which sort instruction uses two levels?',options:['Sort Item type A-Z, then Cost low-high','Make all headings bold','Format all income as currency','Count all Shop sales'],answer:0,right:'Correct. That instruction uses a primary and secondary sort.'},{q:'Which tool limits a cell to choices such as Shop or Online?',options:['Data validation','Conditional formatting','AVERAGE','Chart title'],answer:0,right:'Correct.'},{q:'Which tool highlights records that match a rule?',options:['Conditional formatting','Data validation','COUNT','Sort'],answer:0,right:'Correct.'},{q:'A value of 0.375 should be displayed as...',options:['37.5%','GBP 0.38','3750','0.375 rows'],answer:0,right:'Correct. As a percentage, 0.375 is 37.5%.'},{q:'Why format money to 2 decimal places?',options:['It makes monetary values clear and consistent','It changes all formulas to text','It sorts rows automatically','It removes duplicate records'],answer:0,right:'Correct.'}]);

steps.title: Endless Practice
steps.content:
Endless practice

Answer 5 in a row correctly to complete this practice.
steps.js:
(function(){var bank=[{q:'Which tool would make Shop entries stand out automatically?',o:['Conditional formatting','Data validation','MIN','Freeze panes'],a:0},{q:'Which tool would restrict entries to Online or Shop?',o:['Data validation list','SUMIF','Sort descending','Currency'],a:0},{q:'What must stay together when sorting?',o:['The whole row/record','Only the selected column','Only blank cells','Only formulas'],a:0},{q:'Which is a good display for money?',o:['GBP 12.50','12.5 percent','1250','12.50000000'],a:0},{q:'Which is a good display for 0.25 as a percentage?',o:['25%','0.25p','GBP 0.25','2500%'],a:0},{q:'A task says sort Item type A-Z. Binder and Diary: which comes first?',o:['Binder','Diary','The highest cost','The newest row'],a:0},{q:'Why are consistent choices important for COUNTIF?',o:['COUNTIF matches exact criteria text','COUNTIF ignores all text','COUNTIF creates charts','COUNTIF only works on dates'],a:0}];var solved=0,c=document.getElementById('l4-endless');function pick(){return bank[Math.floor(Math.random()*bank.length)];}function render(){var q=pick();var opts=q.o.map(function(x,i){return{x:x,i:i};}).sort(function(){return Math.random()-0.5;});c.innerHTML='

Correct in a row: '+solved+' / 5

'+q.q+'

'+opts.map(function(o){return '';}).join('')+'
';c.querySelectorAll('button').forEach(function(btn){btn.onclick=function(){var fb=c.querySelector('.ex-feedback');if(parseInt(btn.dataset.i)===q.a){solved++;fb.className='ex-feedback ok';fb.textContent=solved>=5?'Practice complete.':'Correct. Next question.';if(solved>=5)__markStepComplete();else setTimeout(render,700);}else{solved=0;fb.className='ex-feedback err';fb.textContent='Not quite - streak reset.';setTimeout(render,1000);}};});}render();})();

quizQuestions.q: When sorting a dataset, why must the whole table be selected?
options: To keep each row's values together

quizQuestions.q: Which tool is best for limiting Sales Method to Shop or Online?
Which tool would highlight all Shop records?
In E7, calculate the total income for all sales using the Income values in D2:D6.
In E8, calculate the total income for Shop sales only.
In E10, count how many records have Online as the sales method.
Which display is most appropriate for a monetary value of 12.5?
A percentage formula gives 0.5131. How should this normally be presented?
